arguments.name: build-catalog-app
arguments.prompt:
You are working in `/tmp/sv-29f9338b0e0d4b699852425622e1686d/UniversityCourseCatalog` — a freshly scaffolded Blazor Web App (.NET 10, static SSR only, interactivity None). Bootstrap is already included.

Build a university course catalog app with these requirements:
1. Browse departments and courses in a searchable, filterable list
2. Course detail pages (description, schedule, credits, instructor bio)
3. Students submit an enrollment request form (name, student ID, course selection) — standard HTML form post, no wizards or real-time validation
4. Homepage shows featured courses and department links
5. No authentication
6. Intranet deployment (fast connectivity)

## Architecture decisions
- Static SSR only (no interactive render modes) — forms use `<EditForm>` with `FormName` and `[SupplyParameterFromForm]` for standard POST handling
- In-memory data service with seed data (no database) — use a singleton `ICatalogService`
- Clean model classes in a `Models/` folder
- Service in `Services/` folder

## What to build

### 1. Models (`Models/` folder)
- `Department` — Id, Name, Code (e.g. "CS"), Description
- `Course` — Id, DepartmentId, Code (e.g. "CS101"), Title, Description, Credits (int), Schedule (string like "MWF 10:00-10:50"), InstructorName, InstructorBio, IsFeatured (bool)
- `EnrollmentRequest` — Id, StudentName, StudentId, CourseId, SubmittedAt, plus any needed validation attributes (`[Required]`)

### 2. Service (`Services/CatalogService.cs`)
- `ICatalogService` with methods: GetDepartments(), GetDepartment(int id), GetCourses(string? search, int? departmentId), GetCourse(int id), GetFeaturedCourses(), SubmitEnrollmentRequest(EnrollmentRequest request)
- `CatalogService` implementation with rich seed data: at least 4 departments (Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics, English) with 3-4 courses each. Make descriptions realistic and multi-sentence. Give each course a unique instructor with a 2-3 sentence bio.

### 3. Pages (in `Components/Pages/`)

**Home.razor** — Replace existing. Route `/`. Show:
- Hero section with university name and tagline
- "Featured Courses" section showing featured courses as Bootstrap cards (title, code, credits, department, link to detail)
- "Departments" section with links to the catalog filtered by department

**Catalog.razor** — Route `/catalog`. Shows:
- Search box (text input) and department filter dropdown, both in a `<form method="get">` that reloads with query params
- Use `[SupplyParameterFromQuery]` for `Search` and `DepartmentId` parameters
- Results as a Bootstrap table or card list showing course code, title, department, credits, schedule, with links to detail page

**CourseDetail.razor** — Route `/courses/{Id:int}`. Shows:
- Course title, code, department name, credits, schedule
- Full description
- Instructor name and bio
- "Enroll Now" button linking to the enrollment form with courseId pre-selected

**Enroll.razor** — Route `/enroll`. Shows:
- `<EditForm Model="Input" FormName="enroll" OnValidSubmit="HandleSubmit" method="post">`
- Fields: Student Name, Student ID (text), Course selection (dropdown of all courses)
- Use `[SupplyParameterFromForm]` for the model, `[SupplyParameterFromQuery]` for optional `CourseId` to pre-select
- On submit, call service and show a success message on the same page
- Use `<DataAnnotationsValidator />` and `<ValidationSummary />`

**Delete `Weather.razor`** — remove the scaffolded weather page.

### 4. Update existing files

**NavMenu.razor** — Replace weather link. Links should be: Home, Course Catalog, Enroll
Use appropriate Bootstrap Icons class names (bi-house-door-fill-nav-menu, bi-book-fill, bi-pencil-square, etc — check what the existing CSS supports or use generic nav-menu icon classes).

**MainLayout.razor** — Update the top-row to show "University Course Catalog" instead of the About link (or keep it minimal).

**Program.cs** — Register `ICatalogService`/`CatalogService` as singleton.

**app.css** (`wwwroot/app.css`) — Add any small custom styles needed for the hero section, course cards, etc. Keep it minimal since Bootstrap handles most styling.

### 5. Important implementation details
- All pages use `@attribute [StreamRendering]` for fast initial load
- The catalog search/filter MUST work via query string parameters and `[SupplyParameterFromQuery]`, not interactive binding
- The enrollment form MUST use Blazor's static SSR form handling (`EditForm` with `FormName`, `[SupplyParameterFromForm]`)
- Include `<PageTitle>` on every page
- Make sure to add `@using UniversityCourseCatalog.Models` and `@using UniversityCourseCatalog.Services` to `_Imports.razor`

After creating all files, run `dotnet build` to verify everything compiles.

+    public List<Department> GetDepartments() => _departments;

+    public Department? GetDepartment(int id) => _departments.FirstOrDefault(d => d.Id == id);
+
+    public List<Course> GetCourses(string? search, int? departmentId)
+    {
+        var query = _courses.AsEnumerable();
+
+        if (departmentId.HasValue)
+            query = query.Where(c => c.DepartmentId == departmentId.Value);
+
+        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(search))
+        {
+            var term = search.Trim().ToLowerInvariant();
+            query = query.Where(c =>
+                c.Title.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
+                c.Code.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
+                c.Description.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
+                c.InstructorName.Contains(term, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
+        }
+
+        return query.ToList();
+    }
+
+    public Course? GetCourse(int id) => _courses.FirstOrDefault(c => c.Id == id);
+
+    public List<Course> GetFeaturedCourses() => _courses.Where(c => c.IsFeatured).ToList();
+
+    public void SubmitEnrollmentRequest(EnrollmentRequest request)
+    {
+        request.Id = _nextEnrollmentId++;
+        request.SubmittedAt = DateTime.UtcNow;
+        _enrollments.Add(request);
+    }
